extra.message: User-controlled input `username` (from query parameter) is directly concatenated into a Jinja2 template string passed to `Environment.from_string()`. Because the input is embedded into the template source itself rather than passed as a render-time variable, an attacker can inject arbitrary Jinja2 template expressions (e.g., `{{ config }}`, `{{ self._TemplateReference__context.cycler.__init__.__globals__.os.popen('id').read() }}`). This leads to full Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) and can result in arbitrary remote code execution on the server. The safe alternative is shown in the commented-out line 17: pass the user input as a render variable, not as part of the template string itself.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')
